key_insight: Peak major stress in the Rubin Observatory M1M3 mirror can be estimated quickly by root-sum-square combining precomputed unit bending-mode stress fields, mirroring the way mode displacements are combined.
engineering_relevance: This enables near-instant stress margin checks and actuator-force optimization for active optics operations without running a full finite-element solve each time.
method: Run NASTRAN to compute unit displacement and unit stress fields for the first 20 bending modes.
Approximate multi-mode corrections by RSS-combining mode contributions instead of recomputing full stress from scratch.
Compare predicted peak principal stresses against full NASTRAN simulations for representative mode combinations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports agreement within a few percent between RSS-based predictions and NASTRAN for peak major principal stress across the glass substrate.
watch_item: The RSS stress rule is validated on representative combinations, so wider operating envelopes and nonlinearity effects should still be checked before operational deployment.
paper_title: Determining stress-based bending mode limits for the Vera C. Rubin Observatory M1M3 active mirror system
